description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a dark gray base color and numerous lighter mineral inclusions scattered throughout its surface, giving it a speckled appearance. The rock has a rough, uneven texture with jagged edges and a generally blocky form, suggesting it may have been broken from a larger formation. One side of the rock features a small, rectangular label with handwritten numbers, indicating it may have been cataloged or studied. The surface is matte and non-reflective, with visible granular details and no signs of polish or smoothing. The rock appears dense and solid, typical of urban landscaping or barrier stones. Its size and mass suggest it could serve as a physical obstacle or boundary marker in an urban environment.
description: A dense, speckled gray rock with rough, jagged surfaces and a catalog label on one side. Its blocky, irregular form and granular texture make it suitable as an urban barrier or landscaping element.
description_view_0: The main face shows a broad, flat surface with a dense pattern of light mineral inclusions on a dark gray background.
description_view_1: This side reveals a narrower, more vertical profile with a similar speckled texture and rough, uneven edges.
description_view_2: This view highlights the catalog label with handwritten numbers, set against the rock's granular surface.
description_view_3: The rock appears more angular and blocky from this angle, with pronounced edges and a consistent speckled pattern.
